column1: # ZAP Baseline Rules — Quality Governance Platform
# Reviewed: 2026-04-07 — suppressions are scope-limited to known-acceptable findings only.
# FAIL is the default for HIGH/CRITICAL; WARNs appear in report but do not block.
# ── Informational / style alerts (non-exploitable in this context) ──

column3: # X-Content-Type-Options header — set by Azure WAF upstream, not app layer
# X-Frame-Options header — CSP frame-ancestors policy covers this
# Incomplete/missing charset — irrelevant for JSON API responses
# Strict-Transport-Security — enforced by Azure Front Door / CDN
# Information disclosure: suspicious XML comment — only in healthz XML probe
# Timestamp disclosure — structured log timestamps intentionally included
# CSP header — warn only; Azure CDN injection path difference from ZAP scan

column1: # ── Authentication-related (ZAP scans unauthenticated baseline; these are expected 401s) ──

column3: # Content-Type mismatch on 401 responses (pre-auth)

column1: # ── Cookie flags (handled at Azure App Service level, not application layer) ──

column3: # Cookie without Secure flag — set by Azure App Service session management, not app code
# Cookie without HttpOnly flag — same: Azure App Service infra cookies
# Storable and cacheable content — API responses include Cache-Control; warn only
# Retrieved from cache — informational; acceptable for static resources
# Server leaks version info — Azure App Service adds Server header; mitigated by WAF
# Server leaks information via X-Powered-By — not set by application layer
# Cookie with SameSite attribute problems — Azure AD B2C auth cookies; external party

column1: # ── Modern CSP/header alerts (already implemented in SecurityHeadersMiddleware) ──

column3: # Permissions-Policy header not set — IS set: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()
# Content Security Policy (CSP) header not set — IS set in SecurityHeadersMiddleware
# Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header missing — IS set to same-origin/cross-origin
# X-AspNet-Version header exposes version — not applicable (Python/FastAPI, not ASP.NET)
# Secure pages include mixed content — N/A for API-only backend
